create.name: CZ
create.synthesis_para: The Ce0.8Zr0.2O2 support (designated as CZ) was synthesised by the co-precipitation method (diluted NH3), by using the corresponding cerium and zirconium nitrate salts. The precipitated solid was dried overnight (110 °C) and calcined in air in an oven at 500 °C for 1 h

create.name: Cu0.2CZ
create.synthesis_para: Ce0.8Zr0.2O2-supported metal catalysts were prepared by incipient wetness impregnation (IWI) of the ceria-zirconia support with the corresponding metal precursors (Cu: 0.20 wt%, Co: 0.19 wt%, Ag: 0.34 wt%, Pt: 0.61 wt%) with the purpose to reach the same molar composition in all the catalysts (being 31.5 μmol·atom/g support). After impregnation, the samples were dried overnight in a muffle at 110 °C and, subsequently, calcined under air at 500 °C for 1 h.

create.name: 10% Mn-CeO2
create.synthesis_para: First, 1.25 g Ce (NO3)6·6H2O and 1.25 g (CH3CO2)3Ce·xH2O were weighed and stirred in 10 ml deionized water until completely dissolved. Then, C4H6MnO4·4H2O was added to the above solution with Mn/Ce atomic ratio of 0,0.05, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25, 0.3, 0.4 and 0.5, and stirred continuously for 0.5 h to obtain the mixed solution (or add C4H6CoO4·4H2O to the above solution at Co/Ce atomic ratios of 0,0.05, 0.1, 0.3, 0.35, 0.4, 0.45 and 0.5. Or add C4H6CuO4·H2O to the solution at Cu/Ce atomic ratios of 0, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.35, 0.4, 0.45, and 0.6). Then 0.3 g of glycine was weighed and stirred in the continuously stirred mixed solution for minutes, and 6 ml of hydrazine hydrate was added to the crucible for another 30 min to obtain the precursor suspension, which was incubated in a muffle furnace at 500 ℃ for 40 min. After the reaction is completed, the furnace is cooled to room temperature naturally, and the target product can be obtained without washing. The products were named as CeO2, 5%, 10%, 15%, 20%, 25%, 30%, 40% and 50% Mn-CeO2 (5%, 10%, 30%, 35%, 40%, 45% and 50% Co-CeO2 or 5%, 10%, 20%, 30%, 35%, 40%, 45% and 60% Cu-CeO2), respectively.

create.name: Cu0·1Ce0·9O2-y
create.synthesis_para: Using Cu(NO3)2, Ni(NO3)2 and CeO2 (all the raw materials were analytical reagent, from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd.) as the raw materials and keeping the molar ratio of (Cu + Ni)/Ce = 1/9, the ternary Cu–Ni–Ce and binary Cu–Ce and Ni–Ce catalysts with different mole ratio of Cu/Ni were prepared by the mechanical ball milling method. The weighted 40 g raw materials and 40 mL deionized water were placed in the ball mill jar (ball to material ratio 5:1), and then ball milled for 6 h with a rate of 500 r/min to get the slurry mixture. After 8 h of drying at 80 °C, the obtained catalyst precursor was calcined up to 400 °C for 4 h. The obtained catalysts were named as Cu0.1-xNixCe0.9O2-y, where x = 0, 0.01, 0.03, 0.05, and 0.1.
